Carl.description: A twenty-seven year old marine electrician and former US Coast Guard member from Seattle. Carl was a trained technician aboard a Coast Guard cutter with significant hand-to-hand combat training. He's a large, athletic man built with natural muscle from his family's athletic heritage. Carl recently ended his relationship with his girlfriend Beatrice and became trapped in a mysterious dungeon dimension after a world-altering transformation. He carries Princess Donut, his ex-girlfriend's prized Persian show cat, through the dungeon as his companion.

events.event: Now equipped with improvised chitin armor (+2 AC). Armed with a rusty short sword.
Allied with Tandy. Equipped with improvised chitin armor (+2 AC) and rusty short sword. Wounded in cricket fight but rested. Former US Coast Guard, capable fighter.
Descended to Floor 2 with Tandy. Threw Princess Donut for killing blow on the Butcher. Currently negotiating with Mongo's crew.
Wore clown skin disguise. Was thrown at Level 85 City Boss. Eyebrows burned off. Dignity destroyed.
Participated in flash mob Mating Dance of Doom atop Prometheus. Mental state: questionable.
Fastball Special'd from warehouse rooftop into crossbow crawler. Eliminated threat. Signature scream. Dignity remains absent.
Fell off warehouse rooftop with Tandy. Unhurt. Watching Tandy glow with swallowed WMD case. Stairwell door just activated.

context: Let me in! It's clear, but it won't be in about five seconds!
How the hell do I do that if you don't open the door!
I took too long? I just got here!
At least tell me how to open these damn loot boxes!
You're tracking my mother's ashes all over...

Carl: Don’t type in all caps. I think Frank is going to try to hurt or
incapacitate us. This is important. Do not react. Do not fight back at
all. Whatever you do, don’t fire a magic missile. Just let it happen.
Trust me on this. I’m going to present an opening. I want to entice him
into action.
Donut: WHAT DO YOU MEAN. FRANK IS AN AMERICAN
HERO.
Carl: Frank is full of shit. He’s lying. I think there might be
somebody else in here with us, and they are getting ready to jump us.

“So,” Odette had said after we watched the scene and the audience’s

while the show restructured.”
“I’m shocked,” Donut said. “Quite shocked.”
The audience laughed.
“King Rust of the Skull Empire, who recently arrived in earth orbit, has

I moved forward to walk alongside Brandon, Chris, and Imani, keeping
my eye on the map. Yolanda kept up the rear, walking with her bow at the
ready. “We’re taking a left two intersections down, right past that safe
room. We’ll have to cross through an infested area, but I’ll firebomb it.”
The mass of red dots was already starting to shift toward us. Thankfully
they were painfully slow. I could take a nap before they’d reach this hall.
“Carl, thank you,” Brandon said.

“Something tells me I wouldn’t be too welcome,” I said.
The show ended with a short description of the issues with the
bathrooms and some bullshit explanation why they had been forced to
implement the rage elemental trap.
“Yeah because piss is worse than blood and guts and whatever that is
that comes out of the grubs,” I said.
It replayed the scene from the beginning of the episode with Jack
whipping it out. It continued, showing Yolanda’s death in gruesome, slow-
motion detail.

Frank whispered something to Maggie, who returned to her chair. He

The name over the woman said Crawler #12,330,800. “Elle McGib.”
“Hi Carl,” she said.
“Hi Mrs. McGibbons. You remembered my name today,” I said, moving
to walk alongside the train.
“This reminds me of the tunnel of love,” she said. “Back in my time, my

Deep breath, I thought. You will not break me. You will not fucking
break me.
“Can that other guy hear me right now?” I asked.
A pause. “No, he cannot,” Mukta said.
“As long as I don’t talk shit about you guys, will there be consequences
for what I say? As long as I participate, I mean?”
Another pause, and this one was longer than before. “They have paid
for your appearance. Nothing else.”
“All right,” I said, “Tell him we want on the show. Now. Before this

He scoffed. “You think you could actually…”
The crowd went absolutely apeshit as the scene ended, and the lights
snapped back on. Donut stood on her hindlegs and raised her two front

“Suck it,” I said.
“Glurp, glurp! Glurp, glurp!” the audience cried.
The Maestro was back to his sneering, condescending self. His unseen

Donut was ignoring all of this as she hopped up and down. “Two shows
in one day, Carl. Two shows! Maybe there is more, too. Do you think we’re
on more shows?”
I sighed. “Donut. You need to stop obsessing over this. It’s dangerous.
It’s going to get us killed.”
“I’m not obsessing,” she said. “What makes you think I’ve been
obsessing?”
“People like you because of what you’ve been doing,” I said. “If you
start playing for the camera, they’re going to like you less. And if you die,

toward their fallen brothers and sisters.
On the map, near the stairs, just a quarter mile away a group of fifteen
red dots sat. I hadn’t noticed them before. I hovered over the icons.
Grub – Pupa Stage.
A counter, currently at just under 10 hours, was ticking away next to
their names.
I remembered the scene from the recap episode. The level 93 mob, after
sliding down the hall, had been hurt by my boom jug. It’d taken almost a
quarter of its life away before it healed itself.

Princess Donut.description: A champion Persian cat with a fluffy, flat face and tortoiseshell coloring. Princess Donut is a show cat who has won numerous ribbons, trophies, and Best-in-Show awards. She is worth more than Carl makes in a year and is temperamental, prone to scratching and spitting. She was originally being cared for by Carl (her owner's ex-boyfriend) when the world transformed. Despite her spoiled nature, she has proven surprisingly capable in the dungeon environment, often acting independently and wandering into situations against Carl's wishes. She displays surprising confidence and agency in exploring the dungeon.

events.event: Allied with Tandy. Has Cracked Glowstone. Discovered she can cast magic (golden light bolts). Very impressed by Tandy's supermodel walk. Calls Carl her 'servant'.
Descended to Floor 2. Delivered killing blow on the Butcher with magic bolt attack. Very proud of herself. Currently claiming the warmest spot in Mongo's safe room.
Won Mongo the Mongoliensis as her pet\! He immediately bit her on the nose. His title is Royal Steed. She's very proud despite the biting.
Received the Hive Mother's Psi-Fang as a trophy. Very pleased with herself. The fang whispers to her.
Cast Animal Friendship on Prometheus. Now rides atop the dragon head like a queen. Has a dragon.
Descended into the Laughing Crypt with the party to confront Grimaldi. Left Prometheus guarding the exit.
Stowed Robe of Eyes for pulse prep. Confiscated Queen's Fang from Mongo. Rated Carl's Fastball landing 7/10.
Fell off rooftop, landed on feet. Prometheus left on rooftop above. Stairwell door activated by first pulse.

context: Mewww! (hissing and spitting)
*knocks vase over*
*discovers fireplace and starts grooming*

Princess Donut has named your party The Royal Court of Princess
Donut.
Princess Donut has changed your title to Royal Bodyguard.
Princess Donut has changed her title to Grand Champion Best in
Dungeon.
“Really?”
Mordecai laughed. “She has the highest combined stat total, so she’s the
designated party leader. As such, she has more control over the party menu.
Don’t worry. The titles don’t mean anything.” He paused, suddenly serious.
“Listen up, kid.

Again, screams.
“Now, let me ask you something,” Odette said. She turned and leaned
in. Her massive breasts barely sloshed over, as if she had some sort of
accordion system connecting her chest with the boobs. “So you might not
realize this, but we’ve been watching you two from the beginning. You
might not be getting any love on the official program, but you two are quite
the sensation. We are so excited to have you on the show tonight. But

“Thank you. I mean that.” I shook it. He then went to his knee and
scratched Donut. “You too, Princess Donut.”
“Of course, sweetheart,” she said, pushing her head against his hand.
“Oh,” Brandon said, “and try to find Agatha if you can. She’s a crazy
asshole, but she’s our crazy asshole.”
I nodded. We turned and headed toward the stairs. I looked at Donut.
“You ready?”
She flipped her tail, and I followed her down the ramp.

“He blew up one of your filthy tractors and smeared three of your
friends all over the cobblestones,” Donut declared. She pointed daintily
south. “They’re in that next neighborhood over if you want to scoop ‘em
up.”
“Goddamnit, Donut,” I whispered. The goblins stared at us with a slack-
jawed gaze of incredulity. “Let me do the talking. I’m the one with the
brand.”
“And I’m the one with the 37 in charisma,” said Donut. “They’re not
going to care.

Like I did with that juicy boss we faced.” She looked at me. “What was his
name? Juicy? Juiced? The Juice? Now that was a fight, wasn’t it?”
The crowd roared their approval.
We talked for about five more minutes. Odette would ask a question
about something that happened. Donut would answer. She had the crowd
eating out of her paws. Odette asked me about my pants. They showed
Donut asking if there was a reward for worst-dressed in the dungeon, and it

The Maestro was playing this off like this was his idea. “Okay, piglets,
my next two guests do not need any introduction. The last you probably saw
of these two, they were stuck in a room with a bunch of old fucks. Are they
going to escape? Spoiler alert, they just got out. We were going to show
what these crazy assholes did to get free, but since they want to suck on it
so bad, we’re going to bring them on early.”
“Glurp, glurp! Glurp, glurp!”
YOU ARE NOW ON AIR.

equipment: Robe of Eyes (adv. Perception, darkvision 120ft, see invisible, see ethereal)

Mordecai.description: A rat hooligan guildmaster of the Tutorial Guild Hall, level 50. Mordecai is a Non-Combatant NPC who stands about a head shorter than Carl. He wears a black vest, blue pants, and well-worn sandals. Mordecai is characterized as wringing his hands nervously and worrying about rules and procedures. He is reminiscent of Master Splinter from Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles. As a Rat Hooligan, he is one of the smartest, fastest, and ugliest of the Rat-Kin race, offering a blend of physical strength and magical capability. He is frustrated about not receiving newsletters and appears anxious about getting in trouble for rule violations.

events.event: Located at the Tutorial Guild Hall, ready to greet new crawlers
Party visited after containing the Remex soul crystal. Mordecai now in Incubus form on Floor 3 - tuxedo, bat wings, Level 50. No longer rat hooligan appearance.

context: Whaddya want? You mobs ain't allowed in here. You know that!
Sorry, crawler. You took too long. I can't open up if there are mobs directly outside. Rules are rules.
It doesn't work that way, kid. We can't just have untrained crawlers wandering around the dungeon, you know.
I shouldn't have let you in. Kid, get your creature! I'll get in trouble if they find out I let a crawler sneak in against the rules.
It's Mordecai, kid. And yes, yes. Of course.
Can you just grab that thing for me? Get him out of here? I think I might be allergic.
What the bloody hell did you do, kid?

Mordecai grunted. “Oh, you should go. Her program is one of the few I
would recommend. Not that they’d give you much of a choice. Speaking of
shows, there’s someone who wants to speak with you. I’ve been asked to
ping her once you showed up here. She’ll arrive in about ten or fifteen
minutes.”
“Another show?” Donut asked. She’d just started combing through her
achievements and boxes. She didn’t seem to have gotten anything
noteworthy except one item. Her boss box contained an Enchanted Fur

photographs of bird-like creatures. The other half of the room was nothing
but a well-worn and oval-shaped gray carpet and an empty desk. A half-
dozen classroom-style chairs lay scattered about. I turned back to the door.
“Is this the only way out of here?” I asked.
“What?” Mordecai asked. The rat wasn’t paying attention to me. He
was focused on the cat.
“Yo,” I said. “Morty. Is this the only exit?”
“It’s Mordecai, kid. And yes, yes. Of course.”

a white, mesh spacesuit. The whole getup was half space-age, half old-
school deep diving suit.
“Good evening, ma’am,” Mordecai said, standing straight.
“Hello, Mordecai,” the fish woman said, her voice amplified through a
little speaker on her outfit. “It’s nice to finally meet you face-to-face.”
There was something about the woman that suddenly made Mordecai
visibly relax, but I couldn’t tell what it was. There might have been an
exchange I couldn’t hear. I just didn’t know.

rather meet a woman, sire children, and then devour those children than
have anything to do with her again.”
“So that’s a no, then?” Donut asked.
Mordecai was no longer a Rat Hooligan. He’d shapeshifted into a much
larger, hairier, and obsidian creature called a Bugaboo. He was like a bear
with no neck, with enormous, owlish eyes, and comically skinny legs. His
long arms were also absurdly thin compared to the rest of the seven-foot-
tall creature.

bottles littered the floor. The upset urn had been fixed, and the ashes all
cleaned up.
“There are six main types of bosses,” Mordecai said after we woke him
up. His voice slurred ever so slightly, and I could smell the alcohol on his
breath. “Neighborhood, Borough, City, Province, Country, and Floor
bosses. There are also others, but those are called elites. You won’t need to
worry about them just yet.”
“So that thing we just fought was the most common type of boss?”
“Yes,” Mordecai said.

relieved, you know what I mean? Since her mom killed her, that means you
didn’t kill her. It would’ve been an accident, but still.”
“Aren’t you tired?” I asked. I was exhausted. I rubbed the bottom of my
foot with the little stone thing. I could just feel the AI watching me. The
recap show would air in a few hours, and I wanted to get some sleep before
that. Afterward, we needed to head out.
Donut plopped herself on my shoulder, settling in to sleep after I
finished with my feet.

“The tusklings are the rulers of the Orcish Supremacy. Stalwart and the
Maestro are princes of the Skull Empire. That’s a whole different system. If
the Orcish Supremacy is a child with a lemonade stand, the Skull Empire is
the Wal-Mart corporation. It’s one of the largest and oldest Syndicate
governments.”
“Yeah, he’d said something about creating the crawl.” I glanced down at
Donut. “He also talked a bit about the ninth floor. How he’s going to be
there.”

The audience cheered. Someone shouted, “Glurp, glurp!” A minute later
the whole audience was glurping.
Odette shook her head in mock disgust.
The show soon ended after that. Like last time, Odette ushered Lexis
out of the room so we could talk for a few minutes.
“Is it worth it,” I asked, indicating the pet. “If he lives, I mean.”
“You should’ve picked the Tummy Acher,” Odette said. “The little
round guy with the mohawk. They’re very friendly and easy to work with.

Squeeks.description: A small, scruffy rat who was being eaten by crickets. Works as a tutorial message runner. Grateful to the party for saving him. Knows dungeon lore.

events.event: Rescued from Giant Cave Crickets by the party. Grateful. Provided Tutorial Map and intel about the Butcher. Located at Cricket Nest.

The Butcher.description: A failed crawler from a previous season, transformed into a dungeon monster. Massive, wears a leather apron, carries an enormous cleaver. Slow but devastatingly powerful - one hit kills. Covered in old grease (vulnerable to fire). Stupid and follows patrol patterns. Guards the CENTER path to the Floor 1 stairs.

Mongo.description: A Male Mongoliensis - a small, psychotic dinosaur with pink feathers, a sharp beak, and tiny T-rex arms. Level 1 pet-class mob bonded to Princess Donut as her Royal Steed. Stubborn, hot-headed, and absolutely vicious. Goes berserk at the sight of enemies. Will try to bite anyone, including allies. Eats corpses with disturbing enthusiasm. Command word: "sic em". Getting him to level 15 would make him incredibly powerful - but keeping him alive that long is nearly impossible.

events.event: Won by Princess Donut. Bit her on the nose immediately. Has been trained to wait for "sic em" before charging.
Nearly killed by centipede poison in first real combat. Stabilized by Carl. Learning that murder-charging has consequences.
Survived near-death by centipede poison. Fully healed in safe room. Still murderously enthusiastic.
Rested and recovered to 7/8 HP.
Received Queen's Fang from Tandy. Now wields the poison dagger in his beak. Somehow even more terrifying.
Long rest at the Broken Column Inn. Recovered to full HP (8/8). Enjoyed the stables - ate something questionable.
Queen's Fang confiscated for pulse prep. Screeched betrayal at the sky. Still angry about it.
Hit ground beak-first after rooftop fall. Tried to bite a cobblestone. Still angry about Queen's Fang confiscation.

context: Mongo the Mongoliensis Level One (Pet of Grand Champion Best in Dungeon Princess Donut) has joined the party. Mongo's title has been changed to Royal Steed.

The stubborn and hot-headed Mongoliensis is not the type of pet to ever be "tamed." The best one can hope for is mutual respect. And even then they still might try to eat you if the fancy strikes them.
While especially powerful, fast, and vicious when they are fully grown at level 15, getting them to level 15 is about as likely as a cheerleader from West Virginia...

If any sort of enemy came anywhere near us, the crazy chicken went absolutely apeshit. He screamed and snapped and tried to kill himself in an attempt to get to the creature. He was like a goddamned psychotic wolverine hopped up on bath salts.
We quickly learned that only one thing stopped the thing from trying to murder me and Donut: seeing another mob.

After a full day, we managed to keep him from just outright charging every time he saw something. He'd snarl and squawk and hop up and down, but he wouldn't plunge headfirst into battle until Donut said the command, which had somehow evolved from "attack" to "sic em."

Imani.description: Human woman, late 20s, shaved head, dark skin, calculating eyes. Carries a crossbow and watches everything with suspicion. Veteran crawler on Floor 2. Skeptical of newcomers but practical - survives by being smart, not strong. Uses weighted chains as a tactical weapon to impede enemies. Reliable in a crisis - tends to wounded allies, coordinates complex maneuvers, and stays until last when retreating. Forms bonds but keeps them guarded.

events.event: Encountered at Floor 2 Junction. Independent crawler, wary of alliances.
Rescued from Cave Stalkers by Tandy's party. Lost most of her crew. Offered alliance to survive Floor 2.
Alliance formed with Tandy's party. Now traveling together through Floor 2.

context: Imani fed Yolanda a health potion, and the woman sat up, wobbling, holding the side of her head.
One\! Imani yanked the door open. The massive, terrifying rage elemental lunged, and as always, teleported away.
Imani's chain, with heavy weights at either end, dropped away, snaking through the chamber. The monster, hopefully, would get its legs tangled in the thin, unbreakable link.
That last platform, connected by Imani's magic chain, remained attached to the rest of the cars.
Imani was the last to go down the ramp. It'd gone quickly. She waved, unsmiling at us. I waved back, and we turned away.
Imani and Yolanda worked together tending to the wounded after the battle.
Imani was level 11 now after the boss fight - a long-term survivor capable of significant growth.

Skitch.description: Twitchy goblin with a hooded cloak. Nervous, fingers always moving, pockets bulging with scavenged loot. Survivor crawler on Floor 2. Curious but easily spooked.

events.event: Spotted lurking near Floor 2 Junction. Trades information and small items.
Rescued from Cave Stalkers. Arm wounded but bandaged. Terrified and fascinated by Mongo.
Now allied with Tandy's party. Claims to know secret paths through the Warrens.

Brennan.description: Human man, stocky build, broken leg from stalker attack. Part of Imani's crew. Grateful for rescue but immobile.

events.event: Alliance formed with Tandy's party. Broken leg - requires carrying to travel.
Leg healed by Tandy's potion. Can walk again. Deeply grateful and loyal to the party.

Grok.description: Massive orc veteran. Killed Floor 1 boss alone. Respects strength and boldness. Now following Tandy's lead against the Hive Mother.

Orc Gate Guard.description: Massive orc warrior with a glowing halberd. Guards the village gates. Hard eyes but not unkind. Warns newcomers about the dangers of the night.

Gryx.description: A scarred goblin bartender at the Desperado Club. Missing one ear. Knows everyone's business and trades information for gold or interesting stories. Speaks in clipped sentences. Has seen a thousand crawlers come and go.

events.event: Met Tandy's party at the Desperado Club. Witnessed Tandy's transformation to Sasquatch. Recommended buying explosives from Pustule.

Pustule.description: Hobgoblin explosives merchant at the Silk Road in the Desperado Club. Level 30. Sells hob-lobbers, dynamite, and bomb-making supplies. Friendly but eccentric - licks her merchandise. Says 'Beware of meteors\!' as a farewell.

Prometheus.description: Ancient Red Dragon freed from Grimaldi's Circus. Scarred, one blind eye, three centuries of captivity. Now loyal to Princess Donut and the party.

events.event: Freed from Grimaldi's Circus. Allied with party. Currently at circus outskirts with the group.

Odette.description: Host of the famous Odette Interview Show, broadcast from a production trailer orbiting Earth. A fish-woman in a white mesh spacesuit - half space-age, half old-school diving suit. Professional, charming, with a massive bosom that barely sloshes when she moves (accordion system). She conducts interviews with notable crawlers, revealing stats, discussing embarrassing moments, and driving audience engagement. Her show is one of the few Mordecai recommends. Known for leaning in dramatically and asking probing questions about crawler adventures.
context: Good evening, ma'am,' Mordecai said, standing straight. 'Hello, Mordecai,' the fish woman said, her voice amplified through a little speaker on her outfit. 'It's nice to finally meet you face-to-face.'

Now, let me ask you something,' Odette said. She turned and leaned in. Her massive breasts barely sloshed over, as if she had some sort of accordion system connecting her chest with the boobs. 'So you might not realize this, but we've been watching you two from the beginning.'

Odette had said after we watched the scene and the audience's uproarious laughter eased. 'So,' Odette had said. 'You are likely unaware of this, but Death Watch Extreme Dungeon Mayhem announced earlier that it was going into hiatus while the show restructured.'
'You should've picked the Tummy Acher,' Odette said. 'The little round guy with the mohawk. They're very friendly and easy to work with.'
The show soon ended after that. Like last time, Odette ushered Lexis out of the room so we could talk for a few minutes.
Mordecai grunted. 'Oh, you should go. Her program is one of the few I would recommend. Not that they'd give you much of a choice.'

interview_show.format: Production trailer orbiting Earth, floating cameras, live audience reactions
features: Leaderboard reveals (views, favorites, levels, money earned)
Stats displayed over crawler heads (audience gasps)
Discussion of recent events (quests, embarrassments, deaths)

Remex the Grand.description: A zombified, featherless skyfowl tethered to an overloading soul crystal. Black swirling orb eyes, hazy smoke rising from body, ethereal worm-like wisps where wings should be. Former husband of Miss Quill, a royal granddaughter who built an army of swordsmen. Tragic figure, centuries old, wants to tell his story before the crystal destroys everything.

events.event: Encountered by Tandy's party. Told his story - wife Quill, the swordsmen army, the overloading crystal. Party chose to listen rather than attack. Crystal pulsing, agitated.
Soul crystal contained by Sheol Glass Reaper Case. Energy streamed into Remex, freeing him from centuries of tethered existence. Chains dissolved. Remex transformed - no longer zombified.
Passed away peacefully surrounded by Tandy's party. Tandy placed a hand on his head as he faded. Final words were gratitude. Died free, unchained, after centuries of torment.
